utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so gloria is a 46 -year-old female today with past medical history of diabetes and back pain and today here for shortness of breath with chf and copd also so gloria tell me what's going on

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i i'm having a lot of trouble sleeping

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and and how long has this been going on for

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: really just for about the past two weeks i i just ca n't ca n't get comfortable you know when i when i lay down in bed i just ca n't ca n't fall

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: is it because you're having you ca n't sleep or you're having shortness of breath or difficulty breathing or what's going on with that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i i feel like i'm just i'm just choking a few minutes after i i lay down to sleep i just ca n't catch my breath

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and are you and how has your pulse ox been your oxygen level been at home i know you your oxygen level here is like ninety two right now in the office which is a little bit on the low side how is how has that been at home

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i can breathe fine

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just when you lay down you get short of breath okay and is it worse when you have you noticed any shortness of breath during the day when you exert yourself when you climb stairs or do other stuff

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't i do n't do any of that usually i just i i sit on the couch and watch my shows

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay fair enough and how about have you noticed any weight gain or swelling in your legs or calves or anything like that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i i ca n't see my ankles anymore and and yeah i i do n't know what's going on with the scale i think the numbers are off because you know suddenly i gained about ten pounds

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: wow okay alright and are you taking i know you were supposed to be taking lasix and we had you on you know diet control to to prevent to limit your salt intake how is that going

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i i do n't know how much salt is in freedoes but you know i i i'm really enjoying those in last weekend we got this really big party and yeah which color is that lasix pill

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah it's it's the white one the round one so it sounds like you're not maybe not taking it as regularly as you should

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no sir i i do n't think i am

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and are you having any chest pain or tightness in your chest or anything like that or not really

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no not really

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: just just when i ca n't breathe good at night you know

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay got it

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so i'll examine you in a second so it's been a couple of weeks are you coughing up anything any fevers with this at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no no fever kinda feel like i'm just bringing a whole bunch of yuck up once in a while though especially first thing in the morning

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright and how have your blood sugars been doing this time i know you're taking the metformin are you checking your accu-cheks how has that been going

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i'm sorry what's an accu-chek

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your blood sugar check are you checking that or not really

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i i i did it a couple of weeks ago

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and it was about it i i think about two thirty it was okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so your hemoglobin a1c last time was seven . five and we had talked about you know trying to improve your diet we had talked about you know we wan na avoid going to insulin but it sounds like it's been a challenge to kinda control the diet and also your blood sugars have been running a little bit high

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah it's it's been a challenge

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and any nausea vomiting or diarrhea or anything like that are you peeing a whole lot or anything like that no

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i'm feeling like crazy

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: ca n't figure out why because i'm not drinking very much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and how is your back then has that been okay i know you're sitting you said you're sitting on the couch a lot watching tv but

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: besides that anything else

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah you know it it just it just really hurts so you know and so that's why i sit on the couch so much

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright no weakness or numbness in your legs right now

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let me examine you now gloria i'm gon na go ahead and do an exam and let's pretend i did my exam i'm just gon na verbalize some of my findings just so i can record this and put it into my my into my chart so neck exam you do have a little bit of swelling in your neck little bit of jvd no bruits your lung exam you have some crackles in both bases and some rales that i can hear and there are a little bit of intermittent wheezing as well on your heart exam you have a two over six systolic ejection murmur you've had that in the past otherwise regular rate and rhythm it does n't feel a regular your belly exam your belly's slightly distended there's no tenderness or guarding or anything like that so that does n't that looks pretty good on your leg exam you do have some one plus pitting edema or actually almost one and a half plus pitting edema in your both of your ankles no calf tenderness negative homans sign that means no blood clots otherwise neurologic exam is normal the rest of your exam is normal so what does this all mean so let me explain that so for the first problem the shortness of breath you know i think you have an exacerbation of your congestive heart failure what i'd like to do is increase your dose of lasix from twenty milligrams to sixty milligrams for the next four days i'm gon na have you check your weights everyday and also i'm gon na go ahead and have you use your albuterol and atrovent we had given you some inhalers in the past i can give you another refill if you need to help with that some of the breathing that you're having the shortness of breath so i'd like to get some of this fluid off you have you check your weights daily we'll have you increase your dose of lasix we'll have you use a breathing treatments and see if that helps your shortness of breath i'd like to have you come back in about couple days actually i wan na see how you're doing and if it does n't get better we may have to increase the dose or send you to the hospital okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't want to go to the hospital doctor

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah so let's try to let's try to use the lasix and let's try to let's try to you know use the breathing treatments and and do that for the second problem the diabetes that we just talked about i like to go ahead and order another blood test another hemoglobin a1c i think we need to your blood sugars have been running a little bit high in the past and we've had a hard time but it's been a while since we checked your last one so i wan na check another one today to see where we are and when we have you come back in a couple days we should have the results back we can then adjust your metformin or we may have to adjust some of the you know add a different medication at that point but but right now i'm gon na order some blood tests we'll have you come back in a couple of days and then we can reassess at that point okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so i had a piece of cake before i came in here is that gon na affect the the lab work

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah we'll probably do a fasting blood sugar we'll we'll order the hemoglobin a1c that should n't be actually matter because that checks long term but if we need your blood sugar may be elevated today i i would n't be surprised alright and i forgot to examine your back by the way so on your back exam you do have some tenderness in the paraspinal areas of your back in the in the lower back mostly no midline tenderness you have good reflexes so i think this is all muscular pain right now for your back pain i'm gon na go ahead and put you on some naprosyn and some flexeril which is a muscle relaxer i'm gon na give you some exercises you can do to help you get off the couch it'll also help your blood sugar and why do n't we have you if that does n't work the the pain medicine and the physical we can start physical therapy and see if that helps okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any questions about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't think so which color pills

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i think it's a white pill and it's round

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: about this big

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: anything else gloria

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no that's it

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright thanks for coming in today

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 46-year-old female with a history of diabetes, back pain, congestive heart failure, and COPD.
Shortness of breath
The doctor asks about the duration of the patient's symptoms.
The patient reports symptoms have persisted for two weeks and she has difficulty sleeping.
The doctor asks if the sleep difficulty is due to shortness of breath.
The patient reports orthopnea.
The patient's oxygen saturation is 92% in the office.
The doctor asks about the patient's oxygen levels at home.
The patient denies dyspnea at home.
The doctor asks if the patient experiences dyspnea on exertion.
The patient reports a sedentary lifestyle.
The doctor asks about weight gain or lower extremity edema.
The patient reports 10 pounds of weight gain and lower extremity edema.
The doctor asks about adherence to Lasix and salt intake.
The patient asks about the appearance of the Lasix pill.
The doctor describes the Lasix pill as a round white pill.
The patient admits to inconsistent medication adherence.
The doctor asks about chest pain or tightness.
The patient denies chest pain or tightness.
The doctor asks about cough and fevers.
The patient reports a productive cough and denies fevers.
Diabetes
The doctor asks about blood sugar monitoring and metformin adherence.
The patient asks for clarification on what an accu-chek is.
The patient reports checking blood sugar a couple of weeks ago.
The patient reports a blood glucose reading of 230.
The patient's previous hemoglobin A1c was 7.5.
The doctor asks about gastrointestinal symptoms and polyuria.
The patient reports polyuria.
Back pain
The doctor asks about the patient's back pain.
The patient reports back pain.
The doctor asks about lower extremity weakness or numbness.
The patient denies lower extremity weakness or numbness.
Physical exam findings include mild JVD, crackles and wheezing in lungs, 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, slightly distended abdomen, and 1.5+ pitting edema in ankles.
The doctor instructs the patient to increase Lasix to 60mg for 4 days, monitor weight daily, and use albuterol and Atrovent inhalers.
The doctor schedules a follow-up visit in a couple of days.
The doctor orders a repeat hemoglobin A1c test.
The patient asks if recent food intake will affect lab results.
Physical exam of the back shows paraspinal tenderness in the lower back.
The doctor prescribes Naprosyn and Flexeril and recommends exercises and potential physical therapy.
The patient asks for clarification on pill appearance.
